entries.definition: وَمِنْهُمْ شَقِيٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ and some of them will be wretched and some happy.
entries.definition_english: وَمِنْهُمْ شَقِيٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ and some of them will be wretched and some happy.

entries.definition:
s-ʿ-y time span; strife, work, effort, to go about (one’s livelihood), to go to; a portion. Of this root, two forms occur 30 times in the Qur’an: سَعى saʿā 20 times and سَعْي saʿy 10 times.
سَعى saʿā [v. intrans. with preps. على and إلى] 1 to endeavour, to strive (2:114) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ مَنَعَ مَساجِدَ اللَّهِ أَنْ يُذْكَرَ فِيهَا اسْمُهُ وَسَعى فِي خَرابِها who could be more inequitable than those who prohibit the mention of God’s name in His places of worship and strive to have them destroyed/deserted? 2 move, walk, move about, run, hasten (20:20) فَأَلْقاها فَإِذا هِيَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعى he threw it down and–lo and behold!–it was a moving snake 3 to do purposefully, to act, to work for, to carry on one’s business (53:39) وَأَنْ لَيْسَ لِلْإِنْسانِ إِلاَّ ما سَعى that man will only have what he has worked towards.

سَعْي saʿy I [n.] endeavour, strife, effort (18:104) الَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا those whose efforts in this world go astray;

438
entries.definition_english:
s-ʿ-y time span; strife, work, effort, to go about (one’s livelihood), to go to; a portion. Of this root, two forms occur 30 times in the Qur’an: سَعى saʿā 20 times and سَعْي saʿy 10 times.

سَعْي saʿy I [n.] endeavour, strife, effort (18:104) الَّذِينَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا those whose efforts in this world go astray;

438
